channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 things to get right with data management for gen AI projects
item.description: According to Kari Briski, VP of AI models, software, and services at Nvidia, successfully implementing gen AI hinges on effective data management and evaluating how different models work together to serve a specific use case.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI 투자 수익 창출? 전문가들이 말하는 ‘AI ROI에의 섣부른 집착이 위험한 이유’
item.description: IT 연구 및 자문 기업 포레스터(Forrester)에 따르면 많은 기업이 AI 프로젝트 시작 후 투자 수익(ROI)를 얻는 데 어려움을 겪고 있지만, 너무 빨리 높은 성과를 요구하면 위험할 수 있는 것으로 나타났다. 포레스터는 일부 IT 리더들이 빠른 가치 창출을 기대하기보다는 실험에 더 많은 시간을 주는 것이 중요하다는 점을 깨닫기 시작하면서, ROI에 집착하는 기업이 조기에 규모를 축소하게 될 수 있다고 예측했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft releases official OpenAI library for .NET
item.description: Microsoft has released its official OpenAI library for .NET, with the goal of ensuring a smooth, reliable integration experience for developers working with OpenAI and Azure OpenAI services in .NET applications. Announced October 1, the now-stable library follows a beta release published in June.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wing and Serve Robotics Partner on Autonomous Delivery Pilot in Dallas
item.description: Dallas pilot expands autonomous last-mile food delivery capabilities using a combination of ground robots and drones, leveraging the strengths of both technologies.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Inside the trials and tribulations of a major ransomware attack
item.description: Like many small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), ‘JPX Construction’ didn’t see itself as a potential target for a ransomware attack. It wasn’t a high-profile organisation. It didn’t seem to hold much high-value data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Exxon Mobil says advanced recycling is the answer to plastic waste. But is it really?
item.description: Despite its seemingly eco-friendly name, the attorney general’s lawsuit denounced advanced recycling as a “public relations stunt” that largely involves superheating plastics to convert them into fuel.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Making use of command history on Linux
item.description: Command history on Linux allows you to review and repeat previous commands with little effort. It is built into the bash shell (included in the shell executable).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arm, Intel, and the vengeance of Newton
item.description: Arm’s attempt to purchase part of Intel’s computer chip business may have been declined, but it once again demonstrates the important role played in the evolution of today’s tech by Apple and its handheld Newton.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Explained: How Salesforce Agentforce’s Atlas reasoning engine works to power AI agents
item.description: Salesforce’s recently launched Agentforce suite of low-code tools is betting on a human-like reasoning engine called Atlas that could be a game-changer in building AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft gives enterprises new reasons to adopt Fabric
item.description: Microsoft is working to make its cloud-based Fabric combined offering of data warehousing, data science, data engineering, and data analytics services more attractive, previewing a host of new features at its first annual European Microsoft Fabric Community Conference last week.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens to prosecute Google over search results
item.description: Former President Trump on Friday threatened to prosecute Google if elected in November, alleging the search engine was “only” showing "bad” stories about him and "good” articles about Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How much are companies willing to spend to get workers back to the office?
item.description: With more and more companies wanting to bring employees back to the office, I pointed out last week the ill-kept secret that there’s a widespread aversion to open office floor plans — or activity-based workplaces, as they have often evolved into today — and that it partially explains why many employ
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zero Trust + AI: A match made in the clouds
item.description: Have any terms in the cybersecurity space generated more buzz than “zero trust” and “AI”? At the risk of sounding like a Magic 8 Ball, my reply is no.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ruby on Rails streamlines deployment with Kamal 2
item.description: Ruby on Rails 8, the latest version of the well-known web app framework for the Ruby programming language, has reached the beta stage. The update promises to make deployment of Rails web apps friendly and usable, without developers needing to rely on a platform-as-a-service (PaaS) cloud setup.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: HP’s new remote support service can even resurrect unbootable PCs
item.description: An unbootable PC is every remote worker’s nightmare. It usually means they need hands-on support that they’re not likely to find in their home office or neighborhood Starbucks. Now there’s hope that even that catastrophe can be corrected remotely.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IT gains open-source tools to manage iPhones, iPads, and Macs
item.description: If you happen to be, or know, an open-source advocate who’s fallen into Apple IT management, here’s some welcome news: all-remote, open-source device management platform Fleet has introduced iOS device support to supplement its existing Mac, Windows, and Linux device management capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Is it possible to save money and run on a public cloud?
item.description: Based on the hype, you would think that enterprises are scurrying off cloud platforms like rats from a sinking ship. The reality is much more nuanced. According to Andover Intel research, only about 9% of enterprises have moved applications out of the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.), chairwoman of the Oversight and Accountability Subcommittee on Cybersecurity, IT and Innovation, is asking for a briefing by Oct. 3. The post Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
